claim: Across 6 PSLQ bases (A=15-element {1,L,pi,pi^2,pi^3,pi^4,log{2,3,5},zeta{3,5},sqrt{2,3,5},euler}; B=7-element elliptic/AGM; C=7-element hypergeometric/Clausen; D=8-element {L,(alg)*pi^2}; Dp=5-element {L,(alg)*pi}; Da=9-element algebraic L^0..L^8) at dps=300, hmax=1e12, residual<1e-80 with mandatory L-coefficient nonzero, ZERO of 5 representative limits matches any basis; pairwise L_i/L_j similarly miss against {1,sqrt2,sqrt3,sqrt5,cbrt2,cbrt3} for all 10 pairs.

First-pass PSLQ produced 5 false-positive HITs in basis D, 5 in Dp, and 10 in pairwise basis E, all corresponding to the trivial integer relation 1+sqrt(5)-2*phi=0 with L-coefficient zero (since phi=(1+sqrt(5))/2). Removing phi from D/Dp/E and adding an explicit L-coefficient nonzero filter eliminated all phantom hits; the corrected pipeline reports 0/35 hits across 5 reps x 7 bases.
